description: CPU-first supercomputer at RIKEN, scheduled with Slurm, reached through shared front-end login nodes. Think CPU and MPI first.

login.auth: SSH key only (no passwords) — register your public key via the portal before first login
login.note: Login nodes are for editing, building, staging, and submitting only — anything heavier belongs in a job.

defaults.note: Set defaults.account (a project ID) in your user config file ~/.hpc-agent/hokusai.json; --account is mandatory.

account.policy: Every job is billed to a project with a finite core-time allowance under a fair-share policy; the balance recovers gradually and moves continuously — read it live with get_projects.

MPC.name: Massively Parallel Computer
MPC.role: the workhorse; most work runs here

LMC.name: Large Memory
LMC.role: jobs needing very large memory on a single host

partition_defaults.note: Asking for more memory than the per-core default share can raise the number of cores you are billed for. Node counts and live occupancy change over time — read them with get_resources.

storage.use: larger datasets and shared project results
storage.note: Opt-in per project, requested through the portal and charged.
Auto-purged once contents age past ~1 week — copy anything to keep back to home/project storage before a job ends.

storage.use: fast per-job scratch for I/O-heavy stages
storage.note: Exists only for the job's duration; wiped when the job ends. Stage inputs in and copy results out before the node releases.

software.primary_toolchain: Intel oneAPI — loading the `intel` module brings the Intel compilers and Intel MPI together (recommended default for building/running MPI on the Xeon nodes)

mpi.alternative: Open MPI — available, but its module CONFLICTS with Intel MPI; load one or the other (module purge before switching)
mpi.launcher: srun — inherits the job's allocation under Slurm, so you don't pass process counts on the launch line, regardless of MPI flavor

software.threads: Threaded code must be told its thread count (OMP_NUM_THREADS), matched to the cores reserved per process, or it runs far slower.
software.containers: Singularity — pull a public image or build your own; GPU container access is enabled automatically for GPU jobs
software.note: Confirm availability and versions live (module avail) — the catalogue evolves.

gpu.note: One GPU also reserves roughly 28 CPU cores. GPU is secondary on HBW2 — reach for it only when a workload genuinely needs one.

network.note: Compute nodes have no direct internet route; outbound connections are relayed through the front-end proxy on port 3128.

job_output.console_log: slurm-<jobid>.out, written in the directory the job was launched from
job_output.scheduling: Jobs are ordered by fair-share priority; a waiting job's reason comes from the scheduler (get_job_status).
